title: Nicole Guajardo
text:
Faculty directory observation
Name: Nicole Guajardo
Snapshot: 2026-07-21
published_titles: Vice Provost of Research and Academic Success, and Dean of Graduate Studies
biography: Dr. Nicole Guajardo earned her BA and MA in psychology from Wake Forest University and her PhD in Life Span Developmental Psychology from West Virginia University. She began her academic career at Idaho State University and then joined the faculty in the Department of Psychology at Christopher Newport University in 2004. Since that time, she has taken on a variety of administrative roles at CNU, including Chair of the Department of Psychology from 2009 to 2010, Executive Assistant to the President for Student Success from 2010 to 2013, Dean of the College of Natural and Behavioral Sciences from 2013 to 2026, and now Vice Provost of Research and Academic Success and Dean of Graduate Studies.
education_entries: Ph D in Psychology, West Virginia University
MA in Psychology, Wake Forest University
BA in Psychology, Wake Forest University
research_interests: Social cognitive development Theory of mind Counterfactual reasoning Relationships among social cognition, executive function, and parent-child interactions
teaching_interests: Child Development Cognitive Development Adult Development and Aging Social Cognition
